input_row.title: Ensure App Service Environment is provisioned with v3 or higher
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal When creating an App Service Environment from the portal, after clicking + Add, ensure that Create App Service Environment v3 is displayed. Default Value: The default version is currently App Service Environment v3. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/environment/overview 2. https://azure.microsoft.com/en-gb/updates?id=App-Service-Environment-v1v2- Retirement-Update-1 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/environment/auto-migration 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/appservice/ase Additional Information: App Service Environment v1 and v2 were retired as of 31 August, 2024. As of September 1, 2024, Azure auto-migrated any remaining App Service Environment v1 and v2 on a best-effort basis. CIS Controls:

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:** 
- The control checks for the Azure App Service Environment version, which is an attribute that can be verified through Azure Resource Manager APIs.
- The Azure CLI documentation has commands that enable checking the version of App Service Environments.
- The audit field suggests that it inherently can be automated, as it mentions the Azure CLI commands. 

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_app_service_environment_version_verified

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- The automated program will utilize Azure Resource Manager APIs or Azure CLI commands to verify the version of App Service Environments.
- Specific resources to enumerate include the App Service Environments (`ase`) in the specified subscriptions and resource groups.
- The exact configuration property to validate includes the `sku` of the App Service Environment to ensure it is `v3` or higher.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria: 
  - Pass if the `sku` contains `v3` or has a higher tier.
  - Fail if the `sku` version is `v1` or `v2`.
- Example Azure CLI command to check the version:
  ```bash
  az appservice environment show --name <ase-name> --resource-group <resource-group-name>
  ```
- The expected output includes the `sku` attributes indicating the version of the App Service Environment.

This automated approach will efficiently gather compliance information across various App Service Environments, helping organizations maintain secure and compliant Azure resources effortlessly.
